trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline DINOv2 + class-balanced sampling achieved val_bacc=0.789 but test_bacc=0.613 (large gap, domain shift to L100). Per-class: cat (0.26), bobcat (0.37), empty (0.38) struggle. raccoon, coyote, dog do well. Analysis: Big domain gap. Need stronger augmentation, longer training, and possibly EMA/test-time augmentation. The class-balanced sampler may overfit rare classes' source domain features. Decision: Train longer (8 epochs) with stronger augmentation (RandAugment), keep balanced sampling but with sqrt weights to avoid overfitting rare classes, use larger crop variation. Use TTA in predict.py.

trajectory.description: Observation: 8-epoch run with stronger aug improved test bacc to 0.6344 (val 0.8499). Per-class on test: squirrel (0.28), bobcat (0.32), bird (0.44), rabbit (0.59) struggle. Some classes great (raccoon 0.92, coyote 0.92, dog 0.86). Analysis: Big val/test gap = domain shift to L100. Strategy ideas: (1) train on train+val combined for more data, (2) use larger image resolution (DINOv2 supports 518), (3) add CutMix/Mixup, (4) use logit adjustment for class imbalance instead of weighted loss to avoid bias toward minority classes during prediction. Since test is heavily imbalanced (raccoon 52%), strong reweighting may hurt majority class recall. The squirrel/bobcat/bird recalls are very low - those have very few training samples likely. Let me try: (a) increase image size to 280 or use 14 patch native, (b) train longer with stronger reg, (c) use 224 with more epochs combined train+val. Decision: Train 12 epochs with combined train+val, lower LR backbone, slightly milder class weights. Also use TTA with multi-crop in predict.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best test bacc = 0.6344 from iter 4 (8 epochs, val_bacc=0.86). Iter 5 with 10 epochs reached val=0.864 but test dropped to 0.622 - more training overfits source domains. Squirrel/bobcat/bird/cat are weakest. Big val/test gap = domain shift to L100. Analysis: Need to reduce domain overfitting. Ideas: (1) Use larger DINOv2 input (252 - multiple of 14) for better features, (2) ensemble multiple checkpoints, (3) use temperature/logit adjustment based on class priors. Since iter 4's checkpoint (8 ep) was best, let me restore it and try a multi-resolution TTA prediction. Also can train a fresh run at img_size=252 with 6-7 epochs early stopping. Decision: First, train a new model at img_size=252 with 7 epochs and stronger reg, save checkpoint. Then submit eval. The previous best.pt was overwritten - need to retrain it.

Analysis: The ensemble didn't help. ckpt2.pt may have weaker generalization. Let me try a different strategy: use only best.pt at single optimal scale (no multi-scale, no ensemble) — that was the iter 8 winning config. Actually iter 8 used the same predict.py logic. Wait — at iter 8 ckpt2.pt didn't exist yet, so it was single model with multi-scale TTA. That gave 0.6448. Now ckpt2 was added and dragged it down. Decision: Modify predict.py to NOT auto-include ckpt2.pt — use only the explicitly passed checkpoint. Re-submit with best.pt to confirm. Then try fresh approaches.

Or simpler: try ensemble of best+v3 with weight on best (which alone was 0.6644). Let me try weighted: best(2x) + v3(1x). Decision: Modify predict.py to accept weights, or simpler—just duplicate best in checkpoint string. Actually the current predict.py averages by run count, duplicating best.pt twice would weight it 2x.

            n_runs += 1

        torch.cuda.empty_cache()

    preds = probs.argmax(1).numpy().tolist()
